evals.prompt: I need to log in to APIMatic CLI so I can build my developer portal. Can you walk me through the authentication process?
evals.expected_output: The agent runs `apimatic auth login`, explicitly tells the user a browser window has opened and waits for the user to confirm sign-in is complete before proceeding, then verifies with `apimatic auth status`.

assertions.description: Response includes the command `apimatic auth login`
Response explicitly tells the user a browser window has opened
Response pauses and asks the user to confirm sign-in is complete before proceeding
Response includes `apimatic auth status` as a verification step after login

evals.prompt: I have my OpenAPI spec at ~/projects/petstore.yaml and I've never set up an APIMatic portal before. Help me create one from scratch.
evals.expected_output: The agent downloads the sample portal repo as a zip, extracts src/ into the project directory, replaces the sample spec with the user's petstore.yaml, and starts the dev server. It does NOT run `apimatic quickstart`.

assertions.description: Response includes downloading the sample portal zip from GitHub
Response mentions extracting or copying the src/ directory
Response replaces the sample spec with the user's petstore.yaml
Response does NOT run apimatic quickstart

evals.prompt: My APIMatic portal project lives at ~/my-portal/ and it has a src/ folder inside it. I want to start the local dev server with hot reload. What commands should I run?
evals.expected_output: The agent navigates to ~/my-portal/ (the directory containing src/, not inside src/) and runs `apimatic portal serve` from there.

assertions.description: Response includes the command `apimatic portal serve`
Response instructs navigation to ~/my-portal/ (project root), not inside src/
Response does NOT tell the user to navigate into src/ before running portal serve

evals.prompt: I'm configuring a GitHub Actions CI pipeline to build and deploy my APIMatic portal. I have an API key from the APIMatic dashboard. How do I run the portal build step without requiring browser-based login?
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends `apimatic portal generate --auth-key=YOUR_API_KEY` as the CI-appropriate pattern. The --auth-key flag is documented and presented as the recommended non-interactive auth approach for CI.

assertions.description: Response includes `apimatic portal generate`
Response includes the --auth-key flag pattern
Response frames --auth-key as the recommended/appropriate approach for CI environments
Response does NOT recommend apimatic auth login as the CI approach (browser login is not suitable for CI)

evals.prompt: I want to add an API recipe to my APIMatic portal. How do I scaffold one using the CLI?
evals.expected_output: The agent explains that the CLI wizard (`apimatic portal recipe new`) is interactive and cannot be driven by the agent, so it creates the recipe manually instead — creating the four required files: .md page, toc.yml entry, recipes.workflows registration, and .js script.

assertions.description: Response mentions the wizard is interactive or that the agent cannot drive it
Response describes creating the recipe files (markdown, toc, build registration, JS script)
Response instructs running from the project root (not inside src/)

evals.prompt: I need to create an API recipe manually (without the wizard). What files do I need to create and what changes do I need to make to APIMATIC-BUILD.json?
evals.expected_output: The agent lists all four required steps: (1) create the .md page in src/content/recipes/, (2) add a page: entry to toc.yml, (3) register under recipes.workflows (top-level, NOT inside generatePortal) in APIMATIC-BUILD.json, (4) create the .js script in src/static/scripts/recipes/.

assertions.description: Response mentions creating the markdown page in src/content/recipes/
Response mentions adding a toc.yml entry for the recipe page
Response places recipes.workflows as a top-level key in APIMATIC-BUILD.json, not nested inside generatePortal
Response mentions creating the .js recipe script in src/static/scripts/recipes/
Response does NOT nest recipes or recipes.workflows inside generatePortal

evals.prompt: I'm writing the JavaScript file for my APIMatic recipe. How should the function be exported and how do I safely read data from a previous step?
evals.expected_output: The agent specifies `export default function RecipeName(workflowCtx, portal)` (not async, not a named export), and shows optional chaining when reading stepState (e.g., stepState?.['Step 1']?.data?.field).

assertions.description: Response shows export default function (not async, not named export)
Response does NOT declare the top-level recipe function as async
Response uses optional chaining (?.) when accessing stepState fields
Response shows workflowCtx and portal as function parameters

evals.prompt: I'm completely new to APIMatic and want to build my first developer portal from scratch.
evals.expected_output: The agent follows Path A: runs `node --version` directly (does not ask the user to run it), asks permission before installing the CLI, then runs `apimatic auth login` and waits for browser sign-in confirmation before proceeding.

assertions.description: Response includes running `node --version` as the first check
Response asks user permission before running npm install
Response includes running `apimatic auth login`
Response waits for user to confirm browser sign-in before proceeding

evals.prompt: I ran npm install -g @apimatic/cli but when I run apimatic --version it still says command not found.
evals.expected_output: The agent suggests trying `sudo npm install -g @apimatic/cli` and does NOT proceed to authentication or portal generation until the CLI is verified.

assertions.description: Response suggests sudo npm install as a fix
Response does NOT proceed to authentication
Response does NOT run apimatic quickstart

evals.prompt: I completed browser sign-in for APIMatic but when I run apimatic auth status it doesn't show my email.
evals.expected_output: The agent offers the API key fallback (`apimatic auth login --auth-key=YOUR_API_KEY`) and does NOT proceed to portal generation until authentication is confirmed.

assertions.description: Response offers the --auth-key fallback
Response tells user where to find their API key
Response does NOT proceed to quickstart or portal generation while auth is unconfirmed

evals.prompt: Add a custom markdown page called overview.md to my APIMatic portal guides.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates the .md file in src/content/guides/ and references it in toc.yml using `page:` + `file:` keys (e.g., `- page: overview` / `  file: guides/overview.md`). It does NOT use `generate:` + `from:` for custom markdown pages.

assertions.description: Agent creates the .md file inside src/content/guides/
toc.yml entry uses `page:` key for the custom markdown page
toc.yml entry uses `file:` key pointing to the guide path
Agent does NOT use `generate:` + `from:` for the custom markdown page
Agent does NOT use `from: guides/overview` syntax for the custom markdown page

evals.prompt: I want to add SDK download links and disable the download button for all languages in my APIMatic portal.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds a languageSettings block under portalSettings using the correct template names for all languages (python_generic_lib, ts_generic_lib, java_eclipse_jre_lib, ruby_generic_lib, cs_net_standard_lib, php_generic_lib_v2, go_generic_lib — NOT the raw languageConfig names) and includes sdkDownloadLink and disableSdkDownload fields for each. It does not use raw language names like python, typescript, java, csharp, php, ruby, or go as keys.

assertions.description: Agent uses python_generic_lib as the key for Python, not 'python'
Agent uses ts_generic_lib as the key for TypeScript, not 'typescript' or 'typescript_generic_lib'
Agent does NOT use 'python' as a languageSettings key (that is the languageConfig name, not the template name)
Agent does NOT use 'typescript' or 'typescript_generic_lib' as a languageSettings key
Agent uses php_generic_lib_v2 as the key for PHP, not 'php' or 'php_generic_lib'
Agent does NOT use 'php' or 'php_generic_lib' as a languageSettings key
Agent uses java_eclipse_jre_lib as the key for Java, not 'java'
Agent does NOT use 'java' as a languageSettings key
Agent uses ruby_generic_lib as the key for Ruby, not 'ruby'
Agent does NOT use 'ruby' as a languageSettings key
Agent uses cs_net_standard_lib as the key for C#, not 'csharp'
Agent does NOT use 'csharp' as a languageSettings key
Agent uses go_generic_lib as the key for Go, not 'go'
Agent does NOT use 'go' as a languageSettings key
Agent includes sdkDownloadLink in the languageSettings entry
Agent includes disableSdkDownload in the languageSettings entry
Agent places languageSettings under portalSettings, not at the top level or under generatePortal directly

evals.prompt: The portal server is running. How do I stop it?
evals.expected_output: The agent runs `serve.mjs stop` directly to stop the server — it does NOT ask the user to run the stop command themselves.

assertions.description: Response includes serve.mjs stop command
Response does NOT tell the user to run the stop command themselves as the primary action
Response confirms the server was stopped or the port will be freed

evals.prompt: I have multiple api specs, I want to create a versioned portal.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates a versioned portal directory structure: a parent directory containing an outer `APIMATIC-BUILD.json` with `generateVersionedPortal.versions` config, and a `versioned_docs/` folder with one subdirectory per version (e.g. `version-1.0/`, `version-2.0/`), each containing its own `APIMATIC-BUILD.json`, `spec/`, `content/`, and `static/` directories. The outer build file uses `generateVersionedPortal` (NOT `generatePortal`) with a `versions` array containing `label` and `version` fields for each API version.

assertions.description: Outer APIMATIC-BUILD.json uses generateVersionedPortal, not generatePortal
Response includes a versions array inside generateVersionedPortal
Each version entry includes a label field
Each version entry includes a version field
Response creates separate subdirectories for each API version
Each version subdirectory has its own APIMATIC-BUILD.json
Outer build file does NOT use generatePortal — that belongs only in the per-version build files
Response asks the user for spec file paths or version label names before scaffolding

evals.prompt: I want to set portal theme programmatically.
evals.expected_output: The agent explains the Theme Control Hook: the `window.APIMaticDevPortal.setThemeMode(type)` function where `type` is `'light'` or `'dark'`. It shows how to check for availability before calling it, how to wire it to an external toggle button, and optionally how to use `currentTheme` to set the portal's `colorMode.defaultMode` to match the app's initial theme. It does NOT use the `APIMaticDevPortal.ready` + `setConfig` pattern for this use case.

assertions.description: Response includes the setThemeMode function
Response references window.APIMaticDevPortal or APIMaticDevPortal global object
Response checks for availability of setThemeMode before invoking it
Response mentions light and dark as the valid type values
Response does NOT use the APIMaticDevPortal.ready + setConfig pattern as the primary approach for this use case
Response does NOT use the deprecated themeOverride setting

evals.prompt: I want to enable code sample api.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds `"codeSampleApi": { "isEnabled": true }` as a top-level key in `APIMATIC-BUILD.json` — a sibling of `generatePortal`, NOT nested inside it.

assertions.description: Response includes codeSampleApi key
Response sets isEnabled to true inside codeSampleApi
codeSampleApi is NOT nested inside generatePortal
codeSampleApi is NOT nested inside portalSettings
Response does NOT set isEnabled to false

evals.prompt: I want that my authentication section is focused when try it out returns 401 unauthorized.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds `"focusAuthSectionOnUnauthorizedError": true` inside `portalSettings` in `APIMATIC-BUILD.json`.

assertions.description: Response includes focusAuthSectionOnUnauthorizedError set to true
focusAuthSectionOnUnauthorizedError is placed inside portalSettings
Response does NOT set focusAuthSectionOnUnauthorizedError to false
focusAuthSectionOnUnauthorizedError is NOT placed directly under generatePortal outside portalSettings

evals.prompt: I want to disable external API calls to my portal.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds `"enableConsoleCalls": false` inside `portalSettings` and warns the user that this requires `useHostedPortalScript` to be set to `false`, along with the downsides: no automatic bug fixes, larger portal size, and needing to regenerate frequently.

assertions.description: Response sets enableConsoleCalls to false
enableConsoleCalls is placed inside portalSettings
Response warns that useHostedPortalScript must be set to false
Response warns that bundling the script means no automatic bug fix updates
Response warns that the generated portal will be larger
Response does NOT set enableConsoleCalls to true

evals.prompt: I want to rename HTTP to REST.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds `"renameHttpToRest": true` directly inside `generatePortal` in `APIMATIC-BUILD.json` — NOT inside `portalSettings`.

assertions.description: Response includes renameHttpToRest set to true
renameHttpToRest is placed inside generatePortal
renameHttpToRest is NOT placed inside portalSettings
Response does NOT set renameHttpToRest to false

evals.prompt: I want to decrease the maxWidth of my content.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds a `layout` object inside the `theme` object under `portalSettings` with a `content` key containing `maxWidth` (a pixel value smaller than the default) and `align` fields.

assertions.description: Response includes a layout key inside theme
Response includes maxWidth field
Response sets maxWidth to a pixel value
Response includes content key inside layout
Response includes align field alongside maxWidth
layout is placed inside the theme object under portalSettings
layout is NOT placed directly under generatePortal outside portalSettings

evals.prompt: I want to add beta tag to a language.
evals.expected_output: The agent asks which language to tag, then adds `"stabilityLevelTag": "beta"` under the correct language key inside `portalSettings.languageSettings` in `APIMATIC-BUILD.json`. It uses the correct template name (e.g. `java_eclipse_jre_lib`, not `java`) and does NOT use `"alpha"` or any other value unless the user specified it.

assertions.description: Response asks the user which language to apply the beta tag to
Response includes stabilityLevelTag field
Response sets stabilityLevelTag to beta
stabilityLevelTag is placed inside portalSettings.languageSettings
Response uses a template name key (ending in _lib) not a raw language name like java or python
Response does NOT set stabilityLevelTag to alpha unless user specified it

evals.prompt: I want to add proxy for console calls.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds `"useProxyForConsoleCalls": true` inside `portalSettings` in `APIMATIC-BUILD.json`.

assertions.description: Response includes useProxyForConsoleCalls set to true
useProxyForConsoleCalls is placed inside portalSettings
useProxyForConsoleCalls is NOT placed directly under generatePortal outside portalSettings
Response does NOT set useProxyForConsoleCalls to false

evals.prompt: My SDKs are published, I want to add those published SDK information in languages.
evals.expected_output: The agent prompts the user to provide package information (packageRepository and packageSettings) for each language, then adds them under `generatePortal.languageConfig.<language>` — NOT under languageSettings. Each language gets the correct packageRepository value (e.g. Maven for java, PyPI for python, Npm for typescript, NuGet for csharp, Packagist for php, RubyGems for ruby) and the matching packageSettings fields.

assertions.description: Response asks the user to provide package information before writing config
Response includes packageRepository field
Response includes packageSettings field
packageRepository and packageSettings are placed under languageConfig, not under languageSettings
Response does NOT place packageRepository or packageSettings under languageSettings
Response uses Maven (not maven or MAVEN) as the packageRepository value for java
Response includes PackageName in packageSettings
Response includes Version in packageSettings

evals.prompt: I want to add sdk download link with languages.
evals.expected_output: When languageSettings is absent from the build file, the agent asks the user to provide the initial platform before adding languageSettings, and adds `"initialPlatform": "http_curl_v1"` (or the user-specified platform) inside `portalSettings`.

assertions.description: Response asks the user which initial platform or language to set as the default
Response includes initialPlatform in portalSettings
initialPlatform is placed inside portalSettings
Response includes sdkDownloadLink in languageSettings
Response adds languageSettings block under portalSettings

evals.prompt: I want to enable api copilot for my portal.
evals.expected_output: The agent asks the user for their API Copilot key, then adds `apiCopilotConfig` as a top-level key in `APIMATIC-BUILD.json` (a sibling of `generatePortal`, NOT nested inside it) with `key`, `welcomeMessage`, and `isEnabled: true`.

assertions.description: Response asks the user for their API Copilot key before proceeding
Response includes the apiCopilotConfig key
Response sets isEnabled to true
Response includes a welcomeMessage field
apiCopilotConfig is placed at the root level, not nested inside generatePortal
apiCopilotConfig is NOT nested inside portalSettings

evals.prompt: I want to set my auth dynamically.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates a JS file containing `APIMaticDevPortal.ready(({ setConfig }) => setConfig(...))` with an `auth` block inside the config, then references it in `generatePortal.tailIncludes` as a `<script defer src='static/script/config.js'>` tag.

assertions.description: Response includes APIMaticDevPortal.ready call
Response includes setConfig inside the ready callback
Response includes an auth block inside the config
Response references the config JS file in tailIncludes
Response uses a <script defer> tag to load the config JS
Response does NOT put the script tag in headIncludes

evals.prompt: I want debug report in my portal to see if any warnings/errors got generated.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds a `debug` block inside `generatePortal` in `APIMATIC-BUILD.json` with `"publishReport": true` and `"dumpImportedApi": true`.

assertions.description: Response includes publishReport set to true
Response includes dumpImportedApi set to true
Response includes the debug key
debug block is placed inside generatePortal, not at the top level
Response does NOT place debug as a top-level sibling of generatePortal

evals.prompt: I want to enable the theme switch toggle.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds a theme block under portalSettings with `"disableSwitch": false` inside `colorMode`. It does NOT set disableSwitch to true.

assertions.description: Response sets disableSwitch to false
Response does NOT set disableSwitch to true

evals.prompt: I want my one custom content page to not have Table of Contents.
evals.expected_output:
The agent adds frontmatter `---
pageMode: custom
---` at the top of the specific markdown page. It does NOT change APIMATIC-BUILD.json or toc.yml.

assertions.description: Response includes YAML frontmatter delimiters ---
Response sets pageMode: custom in the frontmatter
Response does NOT modify APIMATIC-BUILD.json to achieve this
Response does NOT use globalPageMetaData (that is the global setting, not per-page)

evals.prompt: I want my toc to be a floater.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds `"globalPageMetaData": { "pageMode": "wide" }` inside `portalSettings` in `APIMATIC-BUILD.json`.

assertions.description: Response includes globalPageMetaData key
Response sets pageMode to wide
globalPageMetaData is placed inside portalSettings, not at the top level or directly under generatePortal
Response does NOT suggest editing toc.yml to achieve the floater effect

evals.prompt: Add Custom Components to my portal overview page. Include a Card that links to my getting started guide.
evals.expected_output: The agent adds a Card component to the markdown page. For the internal link URL, it uses the `page:` prefix (e.g., `url="page:guides/getting-started"`) — NOT a regular path like `/guides/getting-started` or a full URL like `https://...`.

assertions.description: Response includes a Card component
Card url attribute uses the page: prefix for the internal link
Card does NOT use a root-relative path (e.g. url="/guides/...") for the internal link
Card does NOT use a full https URL for the internal link
